text:
graded 201 MCP servers. 98% are worse than PostgreSQL's one tool.

PostgreSQL: 46 tokens. Grade A+.
the average server: 2,551 tokens. Grade D-.

one tool at 46 tokens beats nearly every server at 10-20K tokens.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

the pattern in almost every MCP server that scores below C:

1. descriptions written for humans
2. no type declarations on params
3. docstring boilerplate embedded verbatim
4. optional params with no defaults

not bad engineering. just wrong audience.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

postgres: 1 tool, 46 tokens, A+.
mysql: 12 tools, 570 tokens, A+.
redis: 10 tools, 392 tokens, A+.

infrastructure engineers write tight schemas. developer-tool wrappers don't.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
